Plant maintains 4-5 rounded leaves. Leaves are dark green and turgid. No significant change in size or posture. Eggshell fragments and sensor module present.

observations_by_image.health_inference: Healthy and stable, color variation attributed to lighting.

overall_changes: The initial state (EARLIEST) showed P2 as bare soil with eggshells and an unregistered yellow pot. The Money Plant cup was empty.
Image [T-5] was a complete blackout, indicating a temporary data loss.
Between [EARLIEST] and [T-4], a small plant (Mexican Mint) was introduced into P2, and the unregistered yellow pot was removed. A sensor module was installed above P2.
Between [T-4] and [T-3], a Money Plant cutting was introduced into the white cup, and the cup was brought into frame.
From [T-3] to [CURRENT], both plants have remained visually stable and healthy, with minor fluctuations in perceived leaf color primarily due to varying ambient lighting conditions across images.

image_to_image_deltas.EARLIEST_to_T-5: Complete loss of visual data (black image).
image_to_image_deltas.T-5_to_T-4: Return of visual data. Introduction of a plant in P2. Disappearance of the unregistered yellow pot. Introduction of a sensor module. White cup not visible.
image_to_image_deltas.T-4_to_T-3: Reappearance of the white cup, now containing a Money Plant cutting. No significant changes to P2 plant.
image_to_image_deltas.T-3_to_T-2: Significant increase in ambient lighting, making plants appear lighter green. No structural changes.
image_to_image_deltas.T-2_to_T-1: Significant decrease in ambient lighting, returning to darker conditions. No structural changes.
image_to_image_deltas.T-1_to_CURRENT: No significant changes in plant health or structure. Lighting remains dark.

vision_report.visual_health_inference: Based on visual evidence, both the Mexican Mint in the black pot (P2) and the Money Plant cutting in the white cup (Unmonitored) appear to be in good health. The Mexican Mint consistently displays turgid, dark green leaves (adjusting for lighting variations), with no signs of wilting, discoloration, or pest damage. The Money Plant cutting also shows a healthy, turgid leaf, indicating successful water propagation. The soil in P2 appears adequately damp in recent images. The eggshell fragments remain on the soil surface, acting as a visible intervention.
anomalies: Image [T-5] being completely black, representing a data acquisition failure.
The unregistered yellow pot present in [EARLIEST] but absent in all subsequent images, indicating its removal.
The sudden appearance of plants in both P2 and the white cup after the initial 'bare' state, indicating recent planting/propagation interventions not captured in the earliest image.
vision_report.narrative_description: The botanical audit reveals a dynamic environment over the past five days. Initially, the designated Mexican Mint pot (P2) contained only bare soil with eggshells, alongside an unregistered pot. A significant data loss occurred on T-5. Subsequently, a healthy Mexican Mint plant was introduced into P2, and a sensor module was installed. Concurrently, a Money Plant cutting was introduced into a white cup for water propagation. Both plants have maintained a consistent state of good health and turgor throughout the observed period, despite fluctuations in ambient lighting that affected perceived leaf color. The soil in P2 appears consistently damp, and the eggshell fragments remain a stable feature. The unregistered yellow pot was removed from the setup after the initial observation.
